predictions.text: Leaders who delegate red-energy tasks to people who find them energizing scale better

dynamics: Solo Shaan episode teaching management techniques. No debate or dialogue - pure instruction/mentorship format. Shaan walks through 9 discrete frameworks with examples. Tone is direct and practical. No back-and-forth with Sam.
frameworks: Doctor House: ask 'what do you see?' instead of providing conclusions to train curiosity
Holiday Party: work backwards from celebration to identify North Star goal
Beer at the Pub: work backwards from failure to surface core risks and chief assumptions
F-Marry-Kill game: surface which 20% of people/initiatives to double down vs cut
Superpower assignment: explicitly tell people their strength and they'll embody it
Tesla Master Plan: write multi-step company roadmap showing how you'll achieve your mission
Calendar audit: color-code green (energizing) vs red (soul-draining) time blocks
Peer groups: form 5-person groups with shared ambitious goal, monthly 3-hour calls
Kickoff document: one-pager with goal, floor/stretch wins, anti-goals, power hour
learnings: Questions train teams to be curious and clever better than spoon-fed answers
Visualizing success (party drawing) shifts mindset from fear/doubt to certainty/confidence
Working backwards from failure uncovers risks better than asking directly
Clarity on what success looks like (floor vs stretch goals) reduces ambiguity
Identifying your anti-goals (what you don't want) is as important as goals
Delegating energy-draining work to people who enjoy it multiplies team output
Peer groups with shared goals create accountability and tactical learning

banger_quotes.quote: Clarity is power - people go into things without being clear what they're doing
Great ideas come in the shower because your brain can relax and connect dots
Identify your anti-goals: what success looks like that you DON'T want
What's red for you might be green for them - delegation by energy alignment

numbers_dropped: Magic number for F-Marry-Kill game: 3 (or 3,000 if company is larger)
Calendar audit: use 3 highlighters (green, red, yellow)
notable: Solo Shaan episode with no guest. Teaching-heavy format. 9 discrete management frameworks presented as unorthodox alternatives to conventional wisdom. Emphasis on curiosity-building, risk-surfacing, and energy-alignment in teams.
